'''Male''' is one of the four [[grammatical genders]] in [[Aelaki]]. It corresponds to the late reproductive life stage of the speaker's species, which are sequential hermaphrodites progressing from [[Child|child]] to [[Female|female]] to male.

== Overview ==

The male gender is used for animate nouns referring to beings in their late reproductive stage. For invertebrates and artefacts that lack a clear biological life stage, gender may be assigned by perceived age or sound symbolism; old or large entities tend to receive male gender.

== Vowel pattern ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Number !! Vowel
|-
| Singular || '''a'''
|-
| Collective || '''æ'''
|}

== Ki syllable table ==

The following table shows the [[Ki syllable|ki syllables]] for male gender across all four persons, in singular, collective, and [[Inanimate|inanimate]]-marked forms:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Person !! Singular !! Reduplication !! Collective !! Inanimate-marked
|-
| 1st person || tha || thatha || thæ || thaf
|-
| 2nd person || ja || jaja || jæ || jaf
|-
| 3rd person || sha || shasha || shæ || shaf
|-
| 4th person || ka || kaka || kæ || kaf
|}

== Relationship to other genders ==

As [[Aelaki]] speakers mature, they transition biologically from [[Child|child]] to [[Female|female]] to male. Nouns tracking a living being normally shift gender to match the being's current life stage. The [[Inanimate|inanimate]] gender stands apart, reserved for non-living and mass nouns.

== See also ==

* [[Child]]
* [[Female]]
* [[Inanimate]]

[[Category:grammatical genders]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
